headline: Deere Upgraded to Hold at Jefferies With $550 Target: Is the Worst Finally Over for This Farm Equipment Giant?
summary: Deere & Company (NYSE:DE) stock got a vote of cautious confidence from Wall Street on Wednesday, as Jefferies upgraded the farm equipment giant to Hold and set a $550 price target. The move signals that one of Wall Street’s influential voices believes the worst of the agricultural downturn may be behind Deere, even if a ... Deere Upgraded to Hold at Jefferies With $550 Target: Is the Worst Finally Over for This Farm Equipment Giant?

headline: Deere Stock Jumps After an Upgrade. Is This the Bottom of the Agricultural Cycle?
summary: Deere  stock was rising Wednesday, thanks to geopolitics and a Wall Street upgrade.  One analyst is feeling more positive about the shares but still has concerns about the agricultural cycle.  Tuesday, Jefferies analyst Stephen Volkmann upgraded Deere stock to Hold from Sell.

headline: Is It Too Late To Consider Deere (DE) After Strong Multi Year Share Price Gains?
summary: If you are wondering whether Deere at around US$576 is still a solid opportunity or if the easy gains are behind it, this article breaks down what the current price may be implying about value. The stock has returned 2.3% over the last week and 23.4% year to date, with a 41.3% return over the past year and 57.7% and 62.4% over 3 and 5 years, while the last 30 days show a 2.3% decline. Recent coverage has focused on Deere as a core name in capital goods and agricultural equipment. Investors...

headline: How Deere (DE) Valuation Stacks Up After Right To Repair Settlement
summary: Right to repair settlement puts Deere’s repair model under the spotlight Deere (DE) has agreed to a US$99 million settlement resolving multidistrict right to repair litigation, and has committed to provide farmers and independent service providers with digital repair tools, manuals, and diagnostic software for ten years. headline: Deere Right To Repair Settlement Reshapes Aftermarket Economics And Investor Focus
summary: Deere (NYSE:DE) has agreed to a $99 million settlement to resolve a high profile class action lawsuit over farmers' right to repair its equipment. The settlement includes a commitment to provide farmers and independent repairers with digital tools and resources needed for equipment repairs for the next decade. The agreement addresses long running concerns about access to diagnostic software and could influence repair practices across the agriculture and heavy machinery sectors. For...
